context.goal: Establish a stable post-reset baseline before functional checking.
stimulus_intent: Assert the detected reset using the inferred polarity.
Release reset conservatively and observe interface stabilization.
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.

context.goal: Validate correct Johnson counting behavior over multiple cycles
stimulus_intent: Release reset if present and observe a small number of conservative clock edges for externally visible progress.
Release reset and allow the counter to evolve for a conservative number of clock edges
timing_assumptions: Do not assume completion before it becomes externally visible.
Observe at least 128 clock cycles to cover a full Johnson sequence

context.goal: Verify that the asynchronous active‑low reset reliably forces Q to zero even when asserted mid‑cycle
stimulus_intent: Assert rst_n low for one clock, then release and observe Q stabilization
timing_assumptions: Allow at least one clock after reset release before checking Q

context.goal: Confirm that no counting occurs while the reset is asserted
stimulus_intent: Hold rst_n low for several consecutive clock cycles
timing_assumptions: Observe Q across multiple clock edges while reset is asserted

generated_files.description: Marks the rendered cocotb test directory as a Python package.
Conservative DUT interface helpers derived from the contract artifact.
Thin environment wrapper that composes interface, timing-safe waits, and coverage helpers.
Structured oracle helper functions rendered from the oracle artifact.
Stable runtime helpers exposed to LLM-filled stimulus and oracle TODO blocks.
Coverage scaffold rendered from plan coverage tags for later execution stages.
Rendered basic cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Rendered edge cocotb tests aligned to structured plan cases.
Phase 4 executable Makefile shell; Phase 5 injects run-time variables and executes it.

render_warnings: Some rendered cases were downgraded to deferred execution because deterministic stimulus was not available: negative_001
Rendered Makefile is an executable Phase 4 shell; Phase 5 injects execution variables and selects the backend.
Task description was provided to the render stage but not used to invent new testbench semantics.
Spec text was provided to the render stage but only upstream artifacts were trusted for code generation.
Deterministic render remains the default mainline; post-render TODO fill is experimental and not required by the benchmark path.
